title: centaurea solstitialis subsp. mitis
intro: Centaurea solstitialis subsp. mitis, also known as the 'Bitter Knapweed', is a subspecies of Centaurea solstitialis, a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Bitter Knapweed include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, and its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
